title: 10+ Best Zoro.to Alternatives to Watch Free Anime in 2026
content: Summary: Need zoro.to alternatives? Here are 11 top replacements to zoro.to for 2026, incl. HiAnime, the official rebrand of Zoro.to, as well as legal replacements. If you've recently tried to visit zoro.to only to be met with a redirect or a broken page, you are not alone. For millions of fans, the site, widely known as ZoroAnime, was the top pick for free anime streaming. It offered a clean interface, soft subtitles, and a massive library free from the clutter common on older platforms. However, zoro.to has undergone a complex rebranding, first transitioning to Aniwatch and more recently evolving into HiAnime. This shifting landscape has left many users with pressing questions: Is the new site safe? Is my watchlist lost forever? Are there better alternatives available? This post will answer those very questions. In addition to identifying the official successor to Zoro.to (where your account and data likely still exist), we have compiled a carefully ranked list of the 11 best zoro.to alternative sites for 2026 to help you find your new favorite anime streaming spot. Tired of Buffering, Ads, or Low-quality Streams? Try VideoProc IVideoProc Converter AI lets you build your own permanent anime library by either downloading or recording episodes in up to 4K quality, ensuring a buffer-free and ad-free viewing experience. Plus, its AI-powered tools can upscale older videos to sharp 4K, and it includes a full toolkit to handle everything from converting and compressing files to making quick edits and managing subtitles. Free Download Free Download Why Did ZoroAnime Shut Down The original zoro.to domain was targeted and shut down by anti-piracy groups, specifically the Alliance for Creativity and Entertainment (ACE). This is a common action they take against large unauthorized streaming sites. As a result, the original domain now redirects to an ACE landing page that warns against piracy and suggests legal streaming options. What Has Replaced Zoro.to HiAnime.to is currently the only official successor to the original zoro.to. The original development team migrated the site's database, including user accounts, watchlists, and comments, first to a temporary site (Aniwatch) and now to HiAnime. This is why users can still log in with their old Zoro credentials on HiAnime. Any other site, app, or domain using the "Zoro" name is an unofficial clone trying to capitalize on the brand's popularity and should be used with caution. 11 Best Zoro.to Alternatives to Watch Free Anime Online Now that zoro.to's original domain is offline, it's time to explore what other platforms have to offer. We've compiled a definitive list of the best replacements to zoro.to, each with its own strengths and weaknesses. Below are our top 11 recommendations for 2026. 1. HiAnime - Best for Account Recovery and Continuity If you're looking for a direct replacement for zoro.to, HiAnime is the answer. After the original platform faced legal challenges, the developers migrated the database to Aniwatch and then to HiAnime.to. It is not just a clone; it is the official rebrand. For most users, HiAnime is the natural next step. Since it shares the same backend, your old login credentials should still work. And if you spent years building a "Plan to Watch" list, your data is preserved here. It also keeps the familiar "Zerc" currency system and community features. The downsides? The auto-translation can sometimes have errors, such as random symbols or misspellings. Occasional downtime may happen, but it rarely lasts more than an hour. Pros: Your username, password, and watch history are integrated Retains the dark-mode aesthetic and customizable "soft-sub" system Active comment sections and Discord integration Cons: Users report a higher volume of pop-ups compared to the original site The auto-translation feature sometimes includes errors 2. Tubi - Best for Safety and Legal Anime Streaming Tubi ranks second on this list for one clear reason: trustworthiness. In a space where many anime sites operate in legal gray areas and often come with malware risks, Tubi stands out as a fully legal and safe alternative to Zoro.to. It is owned by Fox Corporation and runs as an ad-supported streaming service with a solid, steadily growing anime library. For viewers who are tired of dealing with suspicious pop-ups or worrying about device security, Tubi is a much safer option. The platform offers a simple, TV-like viewing experience. There is no subscription required, and you can start watching without creating an account, although signing up does help with tracking shows and watch history. That said, Tubi is not a one-to-one replacement for zoro.to. Its anime catalog is smaller, and it does not carry the latest seasonal episodes as they air. Instead, it focuses more on well-known classics and a selection of modern titles. Pros: No risk of malware or ISP throttling Official masters ensure high video stability Cons: Fewer titles and slower updates for new episodes 3. Ani
